item.title: TRAI Assesses Mobile Network Quality Across Hamirpur & Bilaspur and nearby areas Route under HP LSA
item.dc:creator: Ministry of Communications
item.description: Ministry of Communications TRAI Assesses Mobile Network Quality Across Hamirpur & Bilaspur and nearby areas Route under HP LSA Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 4:05PM by PIB Delhi The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) has released findings of Independent Drive Test (IDT) conducted across Hamirpur & Bilaspur and nearby areas Route under HP LSA, during the month of May 2026, for information of general telecom consumers. The purpose of this drive test is to assess and verify real time quality of mobile network services (both voice & data) provided by Telecom Service…
item.content:encoded:
Ministry of Communications
TRAI Assesses Mobile Network Quality Across Hamirpur & Bilaspur and nearby areas Route under HP LSA
Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 4:05PM by PIB Delhi

The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) has released findings of Independent Drive Test (IDT) conducted across Hamirpur & Bilaspur and nearby areas Route under HP LSA, during the month of May 2026, for information of general telecom consumers. The purpose of this drive test is to assess and verify real time quality of mobile network services (both voice & data) provided by Telecom Service Providers (TSPs). During the IDT, TRAI captures performance of TSPs for key Quality of Service (QoS) parameters like Coverage, Call Drop Rate (CDR), Call Setup Success Rate (CSSR), data Download (DL) and Upload (UL) throughput etc., which are then published to inform Consumers and encourage TSPs to improve their services.

These IDTs have been designed to capture on ground mobile network performance of all TSPs across diverse usages environment like cities, hotspots, public transport hubs, etc. In this type of drive testing, live data and voice sessions are established using SIM cards from all TSPs over 2G, 3G, 4G and 5G networks. Multiple advanced test handsets are used, and the sessions are monitored and analyzed in real-time using advanced Software Systems.

TRAI, through its appointed agency, conducted drive tests across Railway Drive from Chandigarh to Amb - 168.0 Km, Highway Drive - 119.0 Km, City drive – 290.6 Km, Hotspot locations - 08, Walk Test - 2.5 Km and Inter Operator Calling at 01 location during 12th May 2026 to 16th May 2026 in HP LSA. These tests were conducted under the supervision of the TRAI Regional Office Delhi. The observations presented in drive test reports represent the performance of the TSPs on the area / route under test on the day / time of conducting the drive test.

Drive Test Route Map: The following map provides overview of drive test routes indicating Railway Drive, Highway Drive, City drive, Hotspots, Walk tests & Inter-operator calling, as per the legends shown on the map: -

Figure-1: Drive test routes

  

  

Key Parameters Assessed

    1. Coverage Gap: Percentage of samples, for which signal strength observed less than the minimum prescribed signal strength for respective technology (2G/ 3G/ 4G/ 5G).
    2. Voice Services: Call Setup Success Rate (CSSR), Drop Call Rate (DCR), Call Setup Time, Call Silence Rate, Speech Quality (MOS).
    3. Call Silence Instance: Number of call silence instances occurred during the calls.
    4. Data Services: Download / Upload Throughput, Latency, Jitter, Packet Drop Rate.

The overall mobile network performance in Hamirpur and nearby areas route for the key parameters has been summarized below: -

  1. Coverage Gap - The signal strength observed during voice testing on the drive test route in auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G), measured as the number of samples having poor signal strength out of the total samples collected, was 479/16503 for Airtel, 2232/16232 for BSNL, 272/15571 for RJIL and 2401/16460 for VIL. Details of the coverage gaps have been provided in the map of Annexure-I.
  1. Dropped Calls - Dropped calls in auto-selection mode (5G/ 4G/ 3G/ 2G), measured as the number of dropped calls out of the number of successfully established calls, were 0/148 for Airtel, 18/151 for BSNL, 0/148 for RJIL and 1/148 for VIL. Details of the dropped call locations have been provided in the map of Annexure-I.
  2. Call Silence Instance - Call silence instances in Auto-selection mode (5G/4G), measured as the total number of silence instances observed for > 3 seconds out of the total calls established, were 5/143 for Airtel, 12/118 for BSNL, 4/143 for RJIL and 1/145 for VIL. Details of the call silence instance locations have been provided in the map of Annexure-I.
  3. Data Download and Upload Throughput:
      1. Data Download performance (Overall): Average download speed was observed as 171.15 Mbps for Airtel (5G), 11.29 Mbps for BSNL (4G), 243.12 Mbps for RJIL (5G) and 13.51 Mbps for VIL (4G). Detail of Download throughput has been provided in the map of Annexure-I.
      1. Data Upload performance (Overall): Average upload speed was observed as 18.38 Mbps for Airtel (5G), 4.81 Mbps for BSNL (4G), 19.81 Mbps for RJIL (5G) and 6.35 Mbps for VIL (4G). Details of Upload throughput have been provided in the map of Annexure-I.

Locations of Dropped Calls and Call Silence instances can be seen by clicking red dot on the map of Annexure-I.

The overall mobile network performance in Bilaspur and nearby areas route for the key  parameters has been summarized below: -

  1. Coverage Gap - The signal strength observed during voice testing on the drive test route in auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G), measured as the number of samples having poor signal strength out of the total samples collected, was 902/18816 for Airtel, 5774/17483 for BSNL, 658/19006 for RJIL and 6806/18548 for VIL. Details of the coverage gaps have been provided in the map of Annexure-II.
  2. Dropped Calls - Dropped calls in auto-selection mode (5G/ 4G/ 3G/ 2G), measured as the number of dropped calls out of the number of successfully established calls, were 2/170 for Airtel, 29/159 for BSNL, 3/173 for RJIL and 11/170 for VIL. Details of the dropped call locations have been provided in the map of Annexure-II.
  3. Call Silence Instance - Call silence instances in Auto-selection mode (5G/4G), measured as the total number of silence instances observed for > 3 seconds out of the total calls established, were 10/163 for Airtel, 6/100 for BSNL, 4/167 for RJIL and 7/164 for VIL. Details of the call silence instance locations have been provided in the map of Annexure-II.
  4. Data Download and Upload Throughput:
      1. Data Download performance (Overall): Average download speed was observed as 87.49 Mbps for Airtel (5G), 6.92 Mbps for BSNL (4G), 172.19 Mbps for RJIL (5G) and 13.62 Mbps for VIL (4G). Detail of Download throughput has been provided in the map of Annexure-II.
      1. Data Upload performance (Overall): Average upload speed was observed as 11.82 Mbps for Airtel (5G), 3.78 Mbps for BSNL (4G), 14.45 Mbps for RJIL (5G) and 3.96 Mbps for VIL (4G). Details of Upload throughput have been provided in the map of Annexure-II.

Locations of Dropped Calls and Call Silence instances can be seen by clicking red dot on the map of Annexure-II.

The overall mobile network performance in Highway route from Amb to Hamirpur for the key  parameters has been summarized below: -

  1. Coverage Gap - The signal strength observed during voice testing on the drive test route in auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G), measured as the number of samples having poor signal strength out of the total samples collected, was 433/6059 for Airtel, 4070/8372 for BSNL, 120/6097 for RJIL and 2091/5625 for VIL. Details of the coverage gaps have been provided in the map of Annexure-III.
  2. Dropped Calls - Dropped calls in auto-selection mode (5G/ 4G/ 3G/ 2G), measured as the number of dropped calls out of the number of successfully established calls, were 0/30 for Airtel, 18/50 for BSNL, 0/31 for RJIL and 8/35 for VIL. Details of the dropped call locations have been provided in the map of Annexure-III.
  3. Call Silence Instance - Call silence instances in Auto-selection mode (5G/4G), measured as the total number of silence instances observed for > 3 seconds out of the total calls established, were 3/30 for Airtel, 4/43 for BSNL, 1/30 for RJIL and 2/31 for VIL. Details of the call silence instance locations have been provided in the map of Annexure-III.
  4. Data Download and Upload Throughput:
      1. Data Download performance (Overall): Average download speed was observed as 146.26 Mbps for Airtel (5G), 9.57 Mbps for BSNL (4G), 250.71 Mbps for RJIL (5G) and 9.76 Mbps for VIL (4G). Details of Download throughput has been provided in the map of Annexure-III.
      1. Data Upload performance (Overall): Average upload speed was observed as 15.77 Mbps for Airtel (5G), 5.03 Mbps for BSNL (4G), 22.81 Mbps for RJIL (5G) and 5.00 Mbps for VIL (4G). Details of Upload throughput have been provided in the map of Annexure-III.

Locations of Dropped Calls and Call Silence instances can be seen by clicking red dot on the map of Annexure-III.

The overall mobile network performance in Highway route from Hamirpur to Bilaspur for the key parameters has been summarized below: -

  1. Coverage Gap - The signal strength observed during voice testing on the drive test route in auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G), measured as the number of samples having poor signal strength out of the total samples collected, was 36/5892 for Airtel, 1114/5621 for BSNL, 34/5868 for RJIL and 1050/5779 for VIL. Details of the coverage gaps have been provided in the map of Annexure-IV.
  2. Dropped Calls - Dropped calls in auto-selection mode (5G/ 4G/ 3G/ 2G), measured as the number of dropped calls out of the number of successfully established calls, were 0/30 for Airtel, 8/31 for BSNL, 0/30 for RJIL and 2/29 for VIL. Details of the dropped call locations have been provided in the map of Annexure-IV.
  3. Call Silence Instance - Call silence instances in Auto-selection mode (5G/4G), measured as the total number of silence instances observed for > 3 seconds out of the total calls established, were 0/29 for Airtel, 9/32 for BSNL, 1/29 for RJIL and 0/30 for VIL. Details of the call silence instance locations have been provided in the map of Annexure-IV.
  4. Data Download and Upload Throughput:
      1. Data Download performance (Overall): Average download speed was observed as 176.88 Mbps for Airtel (5G), 5.44 Mbps for BSNL (4G), 221.34 Mbps for RJIL (5G) and 10.59 Mbps for VIL (4G). Detail of Download throughput has been provided in the map of Annexure-IV.
      1. Data Upload performance (Overall): Average upload speed was observed as 17.67 Mbps for Airtel (5G), 3.89 Mbps for BSNL (4G), 15.83 Mbps for RJIL (5G) and 5.89 Mbps for VIL (4G). Details of Upload throughput have been provided in the map of Annexure-IV.

Locations of Dropped Calls and Call Silence instances can be seen by clicking red dot on the map of Annexure-IV.

The overall mobile network performance in Railway route from Chandigarh to Amb for the key  parameters has been summarized below: -

  1. Coverage Gap - The signal strength observed during voice testing on the drive test route in auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G), measured as the number of samples having poor signal strength out of the total samples collected, was 312/11112 for Airtel, 1501/10604 for BSNL, 341/11115 for RJIL and 1318/11020 for VIL. Details of the coverage gaps have been provided in the map of Annexure-V.
  2. Dropped Calls - Dropped calls in auto-selection mode (5G/ 4G/ 3G/ 2G), measured as the number of dropped calls out of the number of successfully established calls, were 0/55 for Airtel, 8/55 for BSNL, 0/55 for RJIL and 2/55 for VIL. Details of the dropped call locations have been provided in the map of Annexure-V.
  3. Data Download and Upload Throughput:
      1. Data Download performance (Overall): Average download speed was observed as 165.09 Mbps for Airtel (5G), 12.77 Mbps for BSNL (4G), 195.53 Mbps for RJIL (5G) and 27.16 Mbps for VIL (5G). Detail of Download throughput has been provided in the map of Annexure-V.
      1. Data Upload performance (Overall): Average upload speed was observed as 25.67 Mbps for Airtel (5G), 8.66 Mbps for BSNL (4G), 17.83 Mbps for RJIL (5G) and 15.48 Mbps for VIL (5G). Details of Upload throughput has been provided in the map of Annexure-V.

Locations of Dropped Calls and Call Silence instances can be seen by clicking red dot on the map of Annexure-V.

Details of drive test route and area covered during the IDT are as below: -

  1. City - The areas covered in the city drive are Dhanpur, Jeehan, Karot Khas, Bharti, Paddar, Bharin, Bhota, Aghar and Ladraur etc. under Hamirpur and nearby areas & Harlog, Bhager, Auhar, Bharari, Zakatkhana, Jabal, Thapna, Swarghat, Jamli, Kothipura, Ghaghas, Dehar and Salaper etc. under Bilaspur and nearby areas.
  2. Highway - The area covered in the Highway route are Amb to Hamirpur via Lohara Khas, Jawar, Nadaun, Bharti and Jol Sappar etc. & Hamirpur to Bilaspur via Bharin, Bhota, Dain, Ukhali, Dangar, Dadhol, Naswal, Ghumarwin, Bhager, Kandrour and Upper Nihal etc.
  3. Railway - The area covered in the Railway route is Chandigarh to Amb Via Sahibzada Asngr, Morinda, Rupnagar, Anandpur Sahib, Nangal Dam, Una Himachal.
  4. Hotspot - The hotspot locations, capturing stationary user experience are AIIMS Bilaspur, Bilaspur Bus Stand, DC Office Bilaspur, Dr Radhakrishnan Govt Medical College Hospital, Hamirpur, Government Post Graduate College Bilaspur, ISBT Hamirpur, National Institute of Technology, Hamirpur and Purnam Mall Bilaspur.

e) Walk Test - The walk test conducted on 14th May 2026, covered Main Bazar Hamirpur capturing mobile network behaviors in crowded pedestrian environments.

The findings of this IDT report have been shared with respective TSPs for taking further necessary action at their end. Detailed reports of IDT are made available on the TRAI website at www.trai.gov.in. For any clarification or additional information, an email can be sent to adv.ca@trai.gov or Regional Office of TRAI at Delhi RO can be contacted on telephone no. +91-11-20907772.

 

Click here to see Annexure

NJ/AR

item.title: Telangana’s Next Growth Corridor: How the NH-63 and NH-563 Expansion will Transform Mobility, Trade and Tourism
item.dc:creator: Ministry of Road Transport & Highways
item.description: Ministry of Road Transport & Highways Telangana’s Next Growth Corridor: How the NH-63 and NH-563 Expansion will Transform Mobility, Trade and Tourism Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 4:02PM by PIB Delhi From the temple town of Vemulawada and the historic landscape of Dharmapuri to the agricultural and industrial centres of Nizamabad, Jagtial, Karimnagar and Mancherial, northern Telangana’s growth story is closely tied to the strength of its transport network. As traffic volumes rise and economic activity expands, key stretches of NH-63 and NH-563 have increasingly come under…
item.content:encoded:
Ministry of Road Transport & Highways
Telangana’s Next Growth Corridor: How the NH-63 and NH-563 Expansion will Transform Mobility, Trade and Tourism
Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 4:02PM by PIB Delhi

From the temple town of Vemulawada and the historic landscape of Dharmapuri to the agricultural and industrial centres of Nizamabad, Jagtial, Karimnagar and Mancherial, northern Telangana’s growth story is closely tied to the strength of its transport network. As traffic volumes rise and economic activity expands, key stretches of NH-63 and NH-563 have increasingly come under pressure. The Cabinet’s approval for the four-laning of the Armoor-Jagtial-Mancherial section of NH-63 and the Jagtial-Karimnagar section of NH-563 last month seeks to address that challenge by creating a modern highway network that improves connectivity to pilgrimage destinations, industrial hubs, logistics centres and agricultural markets.

Spread across 190.76 kms, these projects mark a major step in strengthening Telangana’s transport infrastructure. The four-laning of the Armoor–Jagtial–Mancherial section of NH-63 will be implemented under the Hybrid Annuity Model (HAM), while the Jagtial–Karimnagar section of NH-563 will be developed on a Build-Operate-Transfer (Toll) basis. Together, these projects involve an investment of ₹7,597.16 crore, reflecting a strong commitment to enhancing regional connectivity and infrastructure capacity. The upgraded corridors will not only ease traffic movement but also unlock new possibilities for economic growth, tourism and regional development.

Strengthening a Religious and Heritage Tourism Circuit

Roads play a vital role in shaping tourism experiences, particularly in destinations that attract large numbers of pilgrims and visitors throughout the year. The NH-63 and NH-563 upgrades will significantly improve access to some of Telangana’s most popular religious and heritage destinations, such as the Vemulawada Temple, Kondagattu Anjaneya Swamy Temple, Dharmapuri Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy Temple, Kaleshwaram Temple and the historic Nagunur Fort.

The improved highway network will make journeys faster, safer and more comfortable for a vast number of devotees and travellers. It’ll also encourage multi-destination pilgrimage circuits, helping visitors explore several sites during a single trip. The benefits extend beyond tourism alone. Increased visitor footfall creates opportunities for local businesses, hospitality services, transport operators and artisans, generating employment and supporting local economies.

Connecting Telangana’s Economic Growth Centres

Northern Telangana has arisen as an important hub for agriculture, food processing, fisheries and manufacturing in recent years, yet efficient transportation remains critical for sustaining this growth. The upgraded highway corridor will improve connectivity to fisheries and seafood clusters, including Special Economic Zones in Siddipet and Warangal and Mega Food Parks in Nizamabad and Siddipet. For farmers and industries, better roads translate to faster movement of raw materials and finished products, lower logistics costs and greater access to markets.

The project’s alignment with PM Gati Shakti further enhances its significance by connecting multiple economic, social and logistics nodes hubs — contributing to more integrated supply chains and driving infrastructure led growth that accelerates regional development.

From Fields to Freight: A Corridor Powering Prosperity

The districts connected by these highways are deeply linked to Telangana’s agricultural economy. With agriculture a key driver of the local economy, districts along these corridors produce paddy, maize, cotton, turmeric and horticultural crops. Therefore, the region’s prosperity rides on reliable roads. With the four-laning of the Armoor-Jagtial-Mancherial section of NH-63 and Jagtial-Karimnagar section of NH-563, farmers will gain quicker access to consumption markets, mandis, warehouses and processing centres, while businesses involved in food processing and seafood exports will benefit from more predictable transit times. The faster movement of goods will also aid in reducing fuel consumption and vehicle operating costs, improving overall logistics efficiency.

The upgraded corridor will also support the growth of food processing industries by facilitating seamless movement of raw materials and finished products. Improved logistics connectivity will strengthen linkages between farms, food parks, cold storage facilities and processing units, encouraging value addition and reducing wastage of perishable commodities.

Building a Stronger Strategic Transport Network

One of the project’s most significant contributions lies in its role within the larger national highway network. The upgraded corridor will strengthen connectivity with NH-44, India’s longest north-south highway corridor, while also linking key routes such as NH-363 and NH-163G. These connections are crucial for facilitating movement between major urban and industrial centres.

The corridor will also support important regional travel and freight routes, including Nagpur-Hyderabad and Nagpur-Vijayawada connectivity. Upon completion, travel time between Armoor and Mancherial is expected to reduce by approximately 1 hour and 30 minutes, while travel between Jagtial and Karimnagar could be shortened by around 45 minutes. The result will be safer, faster and more reliable journeys across northern Telangana.

At present, traffic movement along both highway corridors is frequently hindered by a series of densely populated towns and expanding urban settlements, leading to congestion, reduced travel speeds and delays for commuters as well as freight vehicles. The Armoor–Jagtial–Mancherial route passes through several built-up areas, including Anksapoor, Korutla, Jagtial, Dharmapuri, Lakshettipet and Mancherial, where increasing traffic volumes often result in bottlenecks and longer journey times. Similarly, the Jagtial–Karimnagar stretch traverses major population centres such as Jagtial, Potharam, Gangadhara and Karimnagar, where traffic congestion has become a persistent challenge. As economic activity and vehicle movement continue to grow across the region, the need for high-capacity, efficient road infrastructure has become increasingly critical.

***

 

GDH/DS

(Release ID: 2286566)

item.title: STATUS ON PUMPED STORAGE PROJECTS
item.dc:creator: Ministry of Power

In addition, 6 PSPs with an aggregate installed capacity of 8,140 MW have been concurred/appraised by the Central Electricity Authority (CEA) and are yet to be taken up for construction Table-II. The measures taken in respect of timely environmental and statutory clearances for Pumped Storage Projects (PSPs) include: Ministry of Environment, Forest…
item.content:encoded:
Ministry of Power
STATUS ON PUMPED STORAGE PROJECTS
Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 3:45PM by PIB Delhi

Presently, 11 Pumped Storage Projects (PSPs) with an aggregate installed capacity of 15,870 MW are under construction Table- I. In addition, 6 PSPs with an aggregate installed capacity of 8,140 MW have been concurred/appraised by the Central Electricity Authority (CEA) and are yet to be taken up for construction Table-II.

The measures taken in respect of timely environmental and statutory clearances for Pumped Storage Projects (PSPs) include:

  1. Ministry of Environment, Forest & Climate Change (MoEF&CC), vide its notification dated 18.05.2023, has enabled appraisal of PSPs under B2 category subject to certain conditions.
  1. The MoEF&CC on 14.08.2023, has issued specific Terms of Reference (TOR) for the proposals involving off stream PSPs wherein collection of baseline data for one season (other than monsoon) is prescribed for off stream closed loop PSPs and collection of baseline data for two seasons (pre-monsoon & post-monsoon) is prescribed for off stream open loop PSPs.
  2. The MOEF&CC on 20.08.2024, has extended the provisions relating to survey in the forest areas for mining projects to other development projects including hydel/PSPs.
  1. MOEF&CC vide letter dated 10.09.2025 has enhanced the number of boreholes for undertaking survey and explorations in the forest area for mining and developmental projects including hydel / PSPs.
  2. Central Electricity Authority (CEA) has revised Guidelines for formulation and concurrence of Detailed Project Report (DPR) of PSPs. Under the revised guidelines, the timeline for concurrence of DPR has been reduced from 90 days to 50 days for all type of PSPs. Further, the clearance of Inter-State Aspects is not required for PSPs.
  3. The Government of India, vide Gazette notification dated 01.08.2025, has revised the capital expenditure limit for schemes related to setting up hydro generating stations to ₹3,000 crore, requiring the concurrence of the CEA. Further, the Government has exempted off-stream closed-loop pumped storage schemes, irrespective of the quantum of capital expenditure, from the requirement of concurrence by the CEA. However, Developers may seek technical guidance from the CEA for projects under exempted category.

Pumped Storage Projects (PSPs) support the integration of renewable energy by storing surplus energy during off-peak periods and supplying power during peak demand. They provide long-duration energy storage, balancing power and ancillary grid services such as frequency regulation, voltage support and black start capability, thereby supporting grid stability, reliability and higher renewable energy penetration.

Central Government has taken a number of measures to encourage greater private sector participation in the development of PSPs as listed below:

  1. Ministry of Power has notified Guidelines to promote development of Pumped    Storage Projects in the country on 10.04.2023.
  2. Budgetary Support to Cost of Enabling Infrastructure, i.e., roads/bridges, railway sidings, ropeways, communication infrastructure and transmission line from power house to nearest pooling point.
  3. To provide a transparent, fair, standardized procurement framework based on open competitive bidding with appropriate risk-sharing between various stakeholders, MoP vide Gazette notification dated 06.02.2025, has notified tariff based competitive bidding (TBCB) guidelines for procurement of storage capacity/stored energy from pumped storage plants.
  1. The Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (CERC), vide its Notification dated 26.06.2025, has provided for 100% waiver of Inter-State Transmission System (ISTS) charges for Pumped Storage Projects (PSPs) for a period of 25 years from the Commercial Operation Date (COD), in respect of projects for which the construction work is awarded on or before 30.06.2028.

item.title: COAL AVAILABILITY FOR THERMAL PLANTS
item.dc:creator: Ministry of Power
item.description: Ministry of Power COAL AVAILABILITY FOR THERMAL PLANTS Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 3:44PM by PIB Delhi The coal (including Lignite) based generation capacity in the country is about 230.8 GW and its share in the total electricity supplied from April to June 2026 is 69.54 %. Further, the maximum generation achieved from such power plants is about 188.8 GW (around 75% of the total generation - 251.4 GW) during non-solar peak demand hours. As on 12.07.2026, 42.8 Million Tonnes (MT) coal stock is available at the Thermal Power Plants, sufficient for 14 days at 85% Plant Load…
item.content:encoded:
Ministry of Power
COAL AVAILABILITY FOR THERMAL PLANTS
Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 3:44PM by PIB Delhi

The coal (including Lignite) based generation capacity in the country is about 230.8 GW and its share in the total electricity supplied from April to June 2026 is 69.54 %. Further, the maximum generation achieved from such power plants is about 188.8 GW (around 75% of the total generation - 251.4 GW) during non-solar peak demand hours.

As on 12.07.2026, 42.8 Million Tonnes (MT) coal stock is available at the Thermal Power Plants, sufficient for 14 days at 85% Plant Load Factor (PLF).  Also, sufficient coal is being received by the Thermal power Plants at present to meet the daily demands.

The scheduled maintenance of thermal units is planned in advance by taking into consideration the demand scenario for different months. It is a standard practice to minimize scheduled maintenance during the peak months. CEA further monitors & permits deferment on case-to-case basis based on the technical parameters of the plant if increased demand necessitates so.

To integrate renewable energy sources in the electricity grid, "Central Electricity Authority (Flexible Operation of Coal based Thermal Power Generating Units) Regulations, 2023" has been notified on 30.01.2023, which mandates all coal based thermal power generating units to achieve minimum technical level of 40% as per the phasing plan specified by the Central Electricity Authority (CEA) from time to time.

Government of India has taken various initiatives to ensure uninterrupted thermal power generation during peak demand periods and are given below:

  1. During FY 2025-26, 9,470 MW Thermal capacity has been added and further 2,260 MW has been commissioned during current Financial Year. Coal supply to the Thermal Power Plants is presently being monitored on daily basis through a joint committee of Ministry of Power, Railways and Ministry of Coal.
  2. Priority to coal rakes and coal dispatch to power houses has also been accorded.
  3. Inter Ministerial Committee (IMC) comprising Chairman, Railway Board; Secretary, Ministry of Coal and Secretary, Ministry of Power is being held regularly to closely review coal supplies to Thermal Power Plants.
  4. About 2,669 MW of Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) & 7,426 MW of Pumped Storage Projects (PSPs) have been commissioned till date to supplement the power generation by the thermal plants during the non-solar hours. These Energy Storage Systems (BESS / PSP) store the excess power generated during solar hours and utilize the stored electricity primarily during non-solar hours.

This information was given by The Minister of State in the Ministry of Power, Shri Shripad Naik, in a written reply in the Rajya Sabha today.

 

****

NR/MD

(Release ID: 2286557)

item.title: Union Minister Dr. Jitendra Singh Calls for Revisiting Pension Rules to Minimise Litigation and Strengthen Pensioners' Welfare
item.category: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions
item.dc:creator: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions
item.description: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions Union Minister Dr. Jitendra Singh Calls for Revisiting Pension Rules to Minimise Litigation and Strengthen Pensioners' Welfare Dr. Jitendra Singh Launches Special Campaign 3.0, Compendium of Case Laws and Pension Litigation Flyer at the 2nd National Workshop on Pension Litigation Government Committed to Building a Zero-Litigation Pension Ecosystem Through Proactive Reforms: Dr. Jitendra Singh Separated Daughters, Family Pension Reforms Reflect Government's Responsive Approach to Social Change: Dr. Jitendra Singh Posted On: …
item.content:encoded:
Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions
Union Minister Dr. Jitendra Singh Calls for Revisiting Pension Rules to Minimise Litigation and Strengthen Pensioners' Welfare
Dr. Jitendra Singh Launches Special Campaign 3.0, Compendium of Case Laws and Pension Litigation Flyer at the 2nd National Workshop on Pension Litigation
Government Committed to Building a Zero-Litigation Pension Ecosystem Through Proactive Reforms: Dr. Jitendra Singh
Separated Daughters, Family Pension Reforms Reflect Government's Responsive Approach to Social Change: Dr. Jitendra Singh
Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 3:39PM by PIB Delhi

Union Minister of State for Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions, Dr. Jitendra Singh today called for a comprehensive review of pension regulations to ensure that evolving social realities are adequately reflected in government policies, stating that many pension disputes arise because existing rules have failed to keep pace with changing societal needs. He emphasised that the ultimate objective of pension administration should be to prevent litigation through timely policy reforms rather than resolving disputes after they reach the courts.

Addressing the 2nd National Workshop on Pension Litigation, organised by the Department of Pension & Pensioners' Welfare at Dr. Ambedkar International Centre, New Delhi, the Minister said the Government is committed to creating a zero-litigation pension ecosystem through simplified rules, improved grievance redressal, stronger coordination among Ministries and proactive identification of recurring issues before they develop into legal disputes.

During the event, Dr. Jitendra Singh launched the Compendium of Case Laws on Pension Litigation, a Flyer on Pension Litigation, and the Special Campaign 3.0 of the Department of Pension & Pensioners' Welfare, aimed at strengthening litigation management, improving service delivery and promoting citizen-centric pension administration.

Dr. Jitendra Singh observed that pension administration must continuously evolve alongside changing social realities. Referring to recent reforms, he highlighted the Government's decision to extend pension benefits to separated daughters by simplifying eligibility conditions, thereby eliminating the need for lengthy legal proceedings in many deserving cases. He said such reforms demonstrate the Government's commitment to making pension policies more humane, inclusive and responsive to contemporary societal requirements.

The Minister also referred to reforms in family pension provisions, noting that outdated regulations inherited from earlier administrative frameworks often created hardship for beneficiaries. He said several such provisions have been revisited over the past few years to ensure that pensioners and their families are not compelled to seek judicial intervention merely to receive legitimate benefits.

Calling for continuous policy review, Dr. Jitendra Singh said recurring litigation often indicates that certain rules require reconsideration. He stressed that when identical grievances emerge from different parts of the country, it should prompt a review of the underlying policy rather than treating each case as an isolated dispute. He urged departments to adopt a research-driven approach towards pension reforms by analysing litigation trends and incorporating stakeholder feedback into policymaking.

The Minister emphasised that grievance redressal should not be viewed merely as disposal of cases but as an important mechanism for policy improvement. Referring to the Government's technology-enabled grievance systems, he said Artificial Intelligence-based platforms have enhanced efficiency, but personal interaction with pensioners remains equally important in understanding their concerns and improving service delivery. He called for a balanced approach combining digital governance with human-centric service.

Dr. Jitendra Singh further stressed the need to simplify pension rules, strengthen outreach through digital platforms and social media, and improve awareness among pensioners regarding their entitlements. He also underlined the importance of sensitising officials and legal functionaries to the rationale behind policy provisions so that avoidable litigation can be minimised.

Highlighting the growing importance of pension administration, the Minister observed that the number of pensioners has now exceeded the number of serving Central Government employees. He said retired employees continue to possess valuable knowledge, expertise and experience and can make meaningful contributions towards the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047, making their welfare an important national priority.

Earlier, welcoming the participants, Dhrubajyoti Sengupta, Joint Secretary, Department of Pension & Pensioners' Welfare, said the Department's vision is centred on improving the ease of life of pensioners through policy reforms, digital governance and effective litigation management. He informed that litigation cases on the Department's Litigation Information Management System (LIMS) have declined by nearly 20 per cent over the past year, reflecting improved coordination among Ministries, legal counsels and departments.

The Joint Secretary further highlighted several digital governance initiatives undertaken by the Department. He informed that 1.92 crore Digital Life Certificates (DLCs) have been generated, while the average time for disposal of pension grievances through the CPENGRAMS portal has been reduced to less than 20 days. He also noted that over 60,000 pension applications have already been processed through the newly introduced single sign-on, e-signed pension application system, with the Department working towards the vision of "One Pensioner, One Portal."

Concluding his address, Dr Jitendra Singh said that effective pension litigation management is not merely a legal exercise but an important component of citizen-centric governance. He expressed confidence that sustained policy reforms, simplified procedures, timely grievance redressal and collaborative engagement among all stakeholders would significantly reduce pension-related litigation while ensuring greater dignity and welfare for pensioners.

The workshop was attended by Ms. Nivedita Shukla Verma, Secretary, Department of Pension & Pensioners' Welfare; Smt. Sukriti Likhi, Secretary, Department of Ex-Servicemen Welfare; Dr. Rajiv Mani, Secretary, Department of Legal Affairs; and R. Venkataramani, Ld. Attorney General for India. The programme featured two technical sessions on pension litigation before tribunals and courts, litigation management, standardisation of case laws, and policy interventions aimed at minimising avoidable pension-related disputes.


****

NKR/SR/SK

(Release ID: 2286556)

item.title: TRAI Assesses Mobile Network Quality Across city drive in Virudnagar and Tenkasi districts on Tamil Nadu LSA
item.dc:creator: Ministry of Communications
item.description: Ministry of Communications TRAI Assesses Mobile Network Quality Across city drive in Virudnagar and Tenkasi districts on Tamil Nadu LSA Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 3:34PM by PIB Delhi The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) has released findings of Independent Drive Test (IDT) conducted across city drive in Virudnagar and Tenkasi districts in Tamil Nadu LSA., during the month of June 2026, for information of general telecom consumers. The purpose of this drive test is to assess and verify real time quality of mobile network services (both voice & data) provided by…
item.content:encoded:
Ministry of Communications
TRAI Assesses Mobile Network Quality Across city drive in Virudnagar and Tenkasi districts on Tamil Nadu LSA
Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 3:34PM by PIB Delhi

The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) has released findings of Independent Drive Test (IDT) conducted across city drive in Virudnagar and Tenkasi districts in Tamil Nadu LSA., during the month of June 2026, for information of general telecom consumers. The purpose of this drive test is to assess and verify real time quality of mobile network services (both voice & data) provided by Telecom Service Providers (TSPs). During the IDT, TRAI captures performance of TSPs for key Quality of Service (QoS) parameters like Coverage, Call Drop Rate (CDR), Call Setup Success Rate (CSSR), data Download (DL) and Upload (UL) throughput etc., which are then published to inform Consumers and encourage TSPs to improve their services.

These IDTs have been designed to capture on ground mobile network performance of all TSPs across diverse usages environment like cities, hotspots, public transport hubs, etc. In this type of drive testing, live data and voice sessions are established using SIM cards from all TSPs over 2G, 3G, 4G, and 5G networks. Multiple advanced test handsets are used, and the sessions are monitored and analysed in real-time using advanced Software Systems.

TRAI, through its appointed agency, conducted drive tests across City drive 273.1 Kms, Hotspot locations 4 in Virudnagar and Tenkasi districts in Tamil Nadu LSA during 16th June 2026 to 18th June 2026 in Tamil Nadu LSA.  These tests were conducted under the supervision of the TRAI Regional Office Hyderabad. The observations presented in drive test reports represent the performance of the TSPs on the area/ route under test on the day/ time of conducting the drive test.

Drive Test Route Map: The following map provides overview of drive test routes indicating City drive, Inter-operator calling, Hotspots and Walk tests, as per the legends shown on the map:-

Key Parameters Assessed

  1. Coverage Gap: Percentage of samples, for which signal strength observed less than the minimum prescribed signal strength for respective technology (2G/ 3G/ 4G/ 5G).
  2. Voice Services: Call Setup Success Rate (CSSR), Drop Call Rate (DCR), Call Setup Time, Call Silence Rate, Speech Quality (MOS).
  3. Call Silence Instance: Number of call silence instance occurred during the calls.
  4. Data Servies: Download/ Upload Throughput, Latency, Jitter, Packet Drop Rate         

The overall mobile network performance in Hyderabad for the key parameters has been summarised below:-

a)   Coverage Gap – (i) The signal strength observed during voice testing on the drive test route in auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G), measured as the number of samples having poor signal strength out of the total samples collected, was 456/41110 for Airtel, 3466/40362 for BSNL, 911/ 39132 for RJIL and 1552/ 40918 for VIL. Details of the coverage gaps have been provided in the map Annexed.     

    (ii) The signal strength observed during voice testing on the drive test route in auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G), measured as the number of samples having Limited service (No Coverage) out of the total samples collected, was 0/41110 for Airtel, 201/40362 for BSNL,     0/39132 for RJIL and 2/376 for VIL. Details of the coverage gaps have been provided in the map Annexed.

b)   Dropped Calls - Dropped calls, measured as the number of dropped calls out of the number of successfully established calls, were 0/367 for Airtel, 5/331 for BSNL, 0/344 for RJIL and 2/376 for VIL Details of the dropped call locations have been provided in the map Annexed.

c)   Call Silence Instance - Call silence instances, measured as the total number of silence instances observed for > 3 seconds out of the total calls established, were 3/349 for Airtel, 32/332 for BSNL 8/353 RJIL and 4/366 for VIL. Details of the call silence instance locations have been provided in the map Annexed.

d)   Data Download and Upload Throughput:

i)  Data Download performance (Overall): Average download speed was observed as 157.73 Mbps for Airtel (5G/4G), 6.25 Mbps for BSNL (3G), 275.96 Mbps for RJIL (5G/4G) and 26.36 Mbps for VIL (5G/4G/2G). Detail of Download throughput has been provided in the map Annexed.

ii) Data Upload performance (Overall): Average upload speed was observed as 21.94 Mbps for Airtel (5G/4G), 2.98 Mbps for BSNL (3G), 19.06 Mbps for RJIL (5G/4G) and 12.46 Mbps for VIL (5G/4G/2G). Detail of Upload throughput has been provided in the map Annexed.

Locations of Dropped Calls and Call Silence instances can be seen by clicking red dot on the map Annexed.

Details of drive test route and area covered during the IDT is as under:-

  1. City: Virudhunagar, Sivakasi & Tenkasi city and Nearby areas: - Allampatti, misalur, Koorakikundu, Muthulingapuram, Sivakasi, Thiruthangal, Achampudur, Panboli, Shenkottai, Courtallam, Thiraviya Nagar, Veikalipatty, Pavoorchatram, Tenkasi, Ayikudy and Elathur etc.
  2. Hotspots: 1.Kaasi Vishwanathar Temple - Tenkasi ,2. Sivakasi Bus Stand 3. Tenkasi New Bus Stand 4. Virudhunagar New Bus Stand

The findings of this IDT report have been shared with respective TSPs for taking further necessary action at their end. Detailed reports of IDT are made available on the TRAI website at www.trai.gov.in. For any clarification or additional information, an email can be sent to adv.hydreabad@trai.gov.in or Regional Office of TRAI can be contacted on telephone no.  040-23000761.

 

Click here for annexure

*** 

  NJ/AR

(Release ID: 2286531)

The purpose of this drive test is to assess and verify real time quality of mobile network services (both voice & data) provided by Telecom Service Providers (TSPs). During the IDT, TRAI captures performance of TSPs for key Quality of…
item.content:encoded:
Ministry of Communications
TRAI Assesses Mobile Network Quality Across Mumbai LSA
Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 3:34PM by PIB Delhi

The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) has released findings of Independent Drive Test (IDT) conducted across Mumbai LSA, during the month of June 2026, for information of general telecom consumers. The purpose of this drive test is to assess and verify real time quality of mobile network services (both voice & data) provided by Telecom Service Providers (TSPs). During the IDT, TRAI captures performance of TSPs for key Quality of Service (QoS) parameters like Coverage, Call Drop Rate (CDR), Call Setup Success Rate (CSSR), data Download (DL) and Upload (UL) throughput etc., which are then published to inform Consumers and encourage TSPs to improve their services.

These IDTs have been designed to capture on ground mobile network performance of all TSPs across diverse usages environment like cities, hotspots, public transport hubs, etc. In this type of drive testing, live data and voice sessions are established using SIM cards from all TSPs over 2G, 3G, 4G, and 5G networks. Multiple advanced test handsets are used, and the sessions are monitored and analysed in real-time using advanced Software Systems.

TRAI, through its appointed agency, conducted drive tests across City drive - 204.3 Kms, Hotspot locations - 7 in Mumbai city during 10th June 2026 to 12th June 2026 in Mumbai LSA. These tests were conducted under the supervision of the TRAI Regional Office Bengaluru. The observations presented in drive test reports represent the performance of the TSPs on the area/ route under test on the day/ time of conducting the drive test.

Drive Test Route Map: The following map provides overview of drive test routes indicating City drive, Inter-operator calling, Hotspots and Walk tests, as per the legends shown on the map:-

Key Parameters Assessed

  1. Coverage Gap: Percentage of samples, for which signal strength observed less than the minimum prescribed signal strength for respective technology (2G/ 3G/ 4G/ 5G).
  2. Voice Services: Call Setup Success Rate (CSSR), Drop Call Rate (DCR), Call Setup Time, Call Silence Rate, Speech Quality (MOS).
  3. Call Silence Instance: Number of call silence instance occurred during the calls.
  4. Data Servies: Download/ Upload Throughput, Latency, Jitter, Packet Drop Rate           

The overall mobile network performance in Uttara Kannada District for the key parameters has been summarised below:-

a)    Coverage Gap - The signal strength observed during voice testing on the drive test route in auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G), measured as the number of samples having poor signal strength out of the total samples collected, was 102/54857 for Airtel, 17877/23461 for MTNL, 144/54576 for RJIL and 5/54511 for VIL. Details of the coverage gaps have been provided in the map Annexed.

b)    Dropped Calls - Dropped calls, measured as the number of dropped calls out of the number of successfully established calls, were 1/490 for Airtel, 28/203 for MTNL 0/489 for RJIL and 1/491 for VIL. Details of the dropped call locations have been provided in the map Annexed.

c)    Call Silence Instance - Call silence instances, measured as the total number of silence instances observed for > 3 seconds out of the total calls established, were 14/ 481 for Airtel, Not Available for MTNL, 1/475 for RJIL and 1/479 for VIL. Details of the call silence instance locations have been provided in the map Annexed.

d)    Data Download and Upload Throughput:

i)   Data Download performance (Overall): Average download speed was observed as 93.76 Mbps for Airtel (5G/4G), Not Available for MTNL , 237.81 Mbps for RJIL (5G/4G) and 70.69 Mbps for VIL (5G/4G/2G). Detail of Download throughput has been provided in the map Annexed.

ii)  Data Upload performance (Overall): Average upload speed was observed as 25.92 Mbps for Airtel (5G/4G), Not Available for MTNL, 40.92 Mbps for RJIL (5G/4G) and 25.57 Mbps for VIL (5G/4G/2G). Detail of Upload throughput has been provided in the map Annexed.

Locations of Dropped Calls and Call Silence instances can be seen by clicking red dot on the map Annexed.

Details of drive test route and area covered during the IDT is as under:-

a) City - The areas covered in the city drive are Bandra Worli Sealink, Mahim, Dadar, Sion, Antop Hill, Worli, Parel, Sewri, Haji Ali, Mumbai Central, Byculla, Malabar Hill, Girgaon, Mazgaon and Churchgate etc in Mumbai city.

b) Hotspot - The hot spot locations, capturing stationary user experience, are Byculla Railway Station, Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus, Churchgate Railway Station, Dadar Railway Station, Gateway of India, Marine Drive, and Parel Railway Station.      

The findings of this IDT report have been shared with respective TSPs for taking further necessary action at their end. Detailed reports of IDT are made available on the TRAI website at www.trai.gov.in. For any clarification or additional information, an email can be sent to adv.bengaluru@trai.gov.in or Regional Office of TRAI at Bengaluru RO can be contacted on telephone No. +91-80-22865004.

Click here to see annexure

*** 

 

NJ/AR

item.title: MONITORING OF INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECT
item.dc:creator: Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation
item.description: Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation MONITORING OF INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECT Project-wise details of all ongoing infrastructure projects are available in the Flash Report published by the Ministry on the website Regular coordination with stakeholders through monthly review meetings and other communications contribute to evidence-based monitoring Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 3:20PM by PIB Delhi The Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation (MoSPI) is mandated to monitor the status of Ongoing Central Sector Infrastructure Projects costing Rs. 150 crore and…
item.content:encoded:
Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation
MONITORING OF INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECT
Project-wise details of all ongoing infrastructure projects are available in the Flash Report published by the Ministry on the website
Regular coordination with stakeholders through monthly review meetings and other communications contribute to evidence-based monitoring
Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 3:20PM by PIB Delhi

The Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation (MoSPI) is mandated to monitor the status of Ongoing Central Sector Infrastructure Projects costing Rs. 150 crore and above through online portal/ web monitoring system “Project Assessment Infrastructure Monitoring and Analytics for Nation-Building (PAIMANA)”. All line Ministries/ Departments/ Implementing Agencies regularly update the information of projects on PAIMANA portal via APIs (Application Programming Interfaces) and data entry mode.

Accordingly, the project-wise details of all ongoing infrastructure projects are available in the Flash Report published by this Ministry on the website https://paimana-proj.mospi.gov.in/ReportPage

The key features of the PAIMANA portal include: Rich Data analytics/ Output Oriented Reports, Customizable Dashboards/ reports to facilitate the apex level monitoring.

The reasons for time and cost overruns are project-specific, depending on a variety of technical, financial and administrative factors and differ from project to project. However, as reported by the project implementing agencies on portal, the main reasons for delay in the projects are: Land Acquisition issues, Forest/ Wildlife/ Environmental clearances, Law and Order issues, Utility Shifting, Delay in Civil Work and others.

All stakeholders (Infrastructure Line Ministries/ Departments) are regularly being apprised of their project execution on the basis of data analytics available on PAIMANA, for any required intervention in managing the project execution at their end. Further, regular coordination with stakeholders through monthly review meetings and other communications contribute to the evidence-based monitoring. In addition to, the login credentials providing access to the customized dashboards are already shared with all Line Ministries/ Departments, thereby giving them direct access to the portal for review of the project progress.

This information was given by the Minister of State (Independent Charge) of the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation, Minister of State (Independent Charge) of the Ministry of Planning and Minister of State in the Ministry of Culture Rao Inderjit Singh in the Rajya Sabha today.

***


Samrat/

(Release ID: 2286550)

item.title: National Statistical Office under Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation has taken several measures to modernise official statistical system through greater use of digital technologies
item.dc:creator: Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation
item.description: Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation National Statistical Office under Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation has taken several measures to modernise official statistical system through greater use of digital technologies Administrative datasets are being increasingly integrated with sample surveys for preparation of sampling frames and selection of survey samples MoSPI has developed the Statistical Quality Assessment Framework based on the United Nations National Quality Assurance Framework, 2019 to strengthen quality assurance in official statistics To promote…
item.content:encoded:
Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation
National Statistical Office under Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation has taken several measures to modernise official statistical system through greater use of digital technologies
Administrative datasets are being increasingly integrated with sample surveys for preparation of sampling frames and selection of survey samples
MoSPI has developed the Statistical Quality Assessment Framework based on the United Nations National Quality Assurance Framework, 2019 to strengthen quality assurance in official statistics
To promote standardisation of metadata, National Metadata Structure (NMDS 2.0) has been developed and shared with Ministries/Departments and States/UTs with the objective that data and information are uniformly represented
Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 3:19PM by PIB Delhi

The National Statistical Office (NSO), under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI), has taken several measures to modernise the official statistical system through greater use of digital technologies. Computer Assisted Personal Interviewing (CAPI) has been adopted for data collection in surveys using handheld devices with in-built validation checks. Survey operations are supported through centralized servers, real-time monitoring dashboards, GPS tagging of surveyed households and secure electronic transmission of data. Various stages of the survey process, including sample selection, field monitoring, survey administration, validation and data processing, have also been automated. These measures have improved data quality by enabling validation at the point of data collection, reduced the time required for data processing and facilitated faster dissemination of survey results. Administrative records are also being increasingly used in statistical operations, while geospatial technologies are leveraged, wherever feasible, to strengthen survey planning and monitoring.

Administrative datasets are being increasingly integrated with sample surveys for preparation of sampling frames and selection of survey samples. For instance, the GSTN database is used as the sampling frame for the Annual Survey of Incorporated Services Sector Enterprises (ASISSE), while the Census 2011 village directory is used for the rural sector under the Annual Survey of Unincorporated Sector Enterprises (ASUSE). The Annual Survey of Industries (ASI) uses administrative records maintained by the Chief Inspector of Factories and other statutory registration authorities for preparation of its survey frame. Similarly, the Ministry of Corporate Affairs database is used for preparing the survey frame for the Forward Looking Survey of Private Corporate Sector CAPEX Investment Intentions. The use of these administrative datasets has strengthened sampling frames, improved survey efficiency and contributed to the timely compilation of official statistics.

MoSPI has developed the Statistical Quality Assessment Framework (SQAF), based on the United Nations National Quality Assurance Framework (UN NQAF), 2019, to strengthen quality assurance in official statistics. The SQAF provides a comprehensive framework covering all aspects to ensure adherence to United Nations Fundamental Principles of Official Statistics (UNFPOS) including aspects to infuse greater efficiency, reliability and accountability. To promote standardisation of metadata, the National Metadata Structure (NMDS 2.0) has been developed and shared with Ministries/Departments and States/UTs with the objective that data and information are uniformly represented, provide clarity on the data sources, collection methods and enhance discoverability of datasets. A Metadata Web Portal (MWP) has also been launched for hosting metadata, and Ministries/Departments and States/UTs have been advised to disseminate datasets along with their metadata. Further, a Compendium of Datasets and Registries, covering 287 datasets of 43 Ministries/Departments, has been published to improve discoverability and facilitate wider use of official datasets.

MoSPI has taken several steps to enhance public access and dissemination of official statistics. An Advance Release Calendar (ARC) is available on MoSPI website indicating the schedule for release of major statistical publications, thereby promoting transparency and predictability in dissemination. The eSankhyiki Portal serves as a single platform for dissemination of official statistics and currently hosts 29 statistical products, 913 datasets and over 137 million records, supported by APIs, data visualisations and metadata. The Microdata Portal has been revamped to provide access to anonymised unit-level data of more than 189 socio-economic surveys. The Ministry has also modernised its website with AI-enabled features and interactive dashboards and launched the GoIStat Mobile App to facilitate easier access to official statistics. In addition, reports, bulletins, factsheets and unit-level data are disseminated through the Ministry's website. Official statistics are further disseminated through multiple communication platforms such as newspapers, television, radio, digital and social media, podcasts on Sansad TV, exhibitions, collaboration with MyGov, explanatory videos, infographics and other multimedia content to maximize outreach and strengthen public engagement.

This information was given by the Minister of State (Independent Charge) of the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation, Minister of State (Independent Charge) of the Ministry of Planning and Minister of State in the Ministry of Culture Rao Inderjit Singh in the Rajya Sabha today.

***


Samrat/

(Release ID: 2286549)

item.title: FRAMEWORK FOR INDICES
item.dc:creator: Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation
item.description: Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation FRAMEWORK FOR INDICES MoSPI is the nodal Ministry for monitoring National Indicator Framework for Sustainable Development Goals The Indicators along with data generated through various official statistical surveys support evidence-based policy formulation Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 3:16PM by PIB Delhi The Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation (MoSPI) compiles various indices such as Estimates of Gross Domestic Product (GDP), the monthly Consumer Price Index (CPI), and the monthly Index of Industrial Production…
item.content:encoded:
Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation
FRAMEWORK FOR INDICES
MoSPI is the nodal Ministry for monitoring National Indicator Framework for Sustainable Development Goals
The Indicators along with data generated through various official statistical surveys support evidence-based policy formulation
Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 3:16PM by PIB Delhi

The Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation (MoSPI) compiles various indices such as Estimates of Gross Domestic Product (GDP), the monthly Consumer Price Index (CPI), and the monthly Index of Industrial Production (IIP). MoSPI also disseminates a wide range of official statistics and administrative data through household surveys on key socio-economic indicators such as labour force, social consumption, health, and education, which are conducted at regular intervals. These surveys provide evidence on various dimensions of social and economic development that are relevant to assessing the quality of life.

Further, MoSPI is the nodal Ministry for monitoring the National Indicator Framework (NIF) for Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), which includes indicators covering health, education, gender equality, environmental sustainability and other aspects related to citizens’ well-being. These indicators, along with data generated through various official statistical surveys, support evidence-based policy formulation and enable assessment of progress towards improving the overall well-being of citizens.

This information was given by the Minister of State (Independent Charge) of the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation, Minister of State (Independent Charge) of the Ministry of Planning and Minister of State in the Ministry of Culture Rao Inderjit Singh in the Rajya Sabha today.

***


Samrat/

(Release ID: 2286548)

item.title: Department of Legal Affairs Organises 8th BRICS Heads of Prosecution Services Meeting
item.dc:creator: Ministry of Law and Justice
item.description: Ministry of Law and Justice Department of Legal Affairs Organises 8th BRICS Heads of Prosecution Services Meeting Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 2:28PM by PIB Delhi The Department of Legal Affairs, Ministry of Law and Justice, Government of India, under the aegis of India’s prestigious 2026 BRICS Chairship, is organising the virtual 8th Meeting of BRICS Heads of Prosecution Services from 20-21 July 2026. Presided over by the Ld. Attorney General for India, Shri R. Venkataramani, this important official dialogue brings together prosecution heads from member countries. The…
item.content:encoded:
Ministry of Law and Justice
Department of Legal Affairs Organises 8th BRICS Heads of Prosecution Services Meeting
Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 2:28PM by PIB Delhi

The Department of Legal Affairs, Ministry of Law and Justice, Government of India, under the aegis of India’s prestigious 2026 BRICS Chairship, is organising the virtual 8th Meeting of BRICS Heads of Prosecution Services from 20-21 July 2026. Presided over by the Ld. Attorney General for India, Shri R. Venkataramani, this important official dialogue brings together prosecution heads from member countries. The landmark meeting perfectly advances India's overarching BRICS vision of “Building for Resilience, Innovation, Cooperation and Sustainability.” By uniting the prosecution leadership of member countries, the meeting highlights a shared commitment to fostering stable, fair, and predictable frameworks that support economic growth across the entire BRICS bloc.

The virtual meeting will focus on the timely and highly apposite theme, “Exchanging Experiences and Best Practices in Criminal Adjudication from the Prosecution’s Perspective”. Over the two days, senior leaders will discuss modern institutional challenges and exchange vital practices of adjudication. The discussions will carry forward the digital thread initiated in previous forums, moving the concepts of Artificial Intelligence and justice into practical, day-to-day procedures. By examining technology-enabled adjudication, the meeting addresses essential areas such as the digitisation of criminal procedures, the management of electronic evidence, and the implementation of time-bound trials.

A central focus of this year's dialogue is the shared goal of making the criminal justice system more sensitive, transparent, and victim-centric. Member countries will share their unique experiences to improve prosecutorial effectiveness and build greater public trust in prosecution services. The forum acknowledges that tackling complex modern challenges such as transnational financial crimes, cyber threats, and environmental violations requires strong cross-border cooperation. Through close dialogue, the meeting aims to enhance mechanisms for mutual legal assistance and cross-border evidence sharing while promoting professional capacity building for Prosecutors.

By bridging geographical distances through digital technology, this meeting marks a significant step forward in deepening institutional bonds between BRICS nations. The Department of Legal Affairs, Ministry of Law and Justice, Government of India, remains dedicated to exploring practical, cooperative frameworks for smoother cooperation and establishing digital platforms for capacity building. Ultimately, this official meeting underscores India’s proactive leadership in shaping a modern, digitally empowered framework, ensuring a more just, efficient, and cooperative future for all member countries.

****

Pranab/ Sagar

(Release ID: 2286527)

Radhakrishnan administered oath/affirmation today in Rajya Sabha Chamber to nine newly elected/ re-elected Members namely Dr. Mukeshbhai J. Rathwa, Shri Pawan Khera, Shri Mahesh Kewat, Shri James Pangsang Kongkal Sangma, Shri Khiangte Laltluangkima, Dr. Satish Poonia, Shri Prakash Chik Baraik, Ms. Sushmita Dev and Shri…
item.content:encoded:
Rajya Sabha Secretariat
Rajya Sabha Chairman Shri  C.P. Radhakrishnan administers oath/affirmation to newly elected/re-elected Members in Rajya Sabha Chamber today
Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 1:55PM by PIB Delhi

Hon'ble Chairman, Rajya Sabha, Shri C.P. Radhakrishnan administered oath/affirmation today in Rajya Sabha Chamber to nine newly elected/ re-elected Members namely Dr. Mukeshbhai J. Rathwa, Shri Pawan Khera, Shri Mahesh Kewat, Shri James Pangsang Kongkal Sangma, Shri Khiangte Laltluangkima, Dr. Satish Poonia, Shri Prakash Chik Baraik, Ms. Sushmita Dev and Shri Sukhendu Sekhar Ray.

Five Members took oath in Hindi, two in English and two in Bengali. Three Members are from the state of West Bengal and one each from Gujarat, Karnataka, Madhya Pradesh, Meghalaya, Mizoram and Rajasthan.

 

******

RKK

(Release ID: 2286513)

item.title: TEXT OF THE OPENING REMARKS MADE IN THE HOUSE TODAY BY SHRI C. P. RADHAKRISHNAN, HON'BLE CHAIRMAN, RAJYA SABHA
item.dc:creator: Rajya Sabha Secretariat
item.description: Rajya Sabha Secretariat TEXT OF THE OPENING REMARKS MADE IN THE HOUSE TODAY BY SHRI C. P. RADHAKRISHNAN, HON'BLE CHAIRMAN, RAJYA SABHA Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 1:50PM by PIB Delhi CHAIRMAN : Hon'ble Members, on behalf of the entire house and myself I welcome the new members. I also extend a warm welcome to all of you as we begin the 271stSession of the Rajya Sabha. This House has a proud tradition of meaningful deliberations, and I am confident this Session will uphold that legacy. We have 19 sittings before us, and it is our collective responsibility to utilize this…
item.content:encoded:
Rajya Sabha Secretariat
TEXT OF THE OPENING REMARKS MADE IN THE HOUSE TODAY BY SHRI C. P. RADHAKRISHNAN, HON'BLE CHAIRMAN, RAJYA SABHA
Posted On: 20 JUL 2026 1:50PM by PIB Delhi

CHAIRMAN: Hon'ble Members, on behalf of the entire house and myself I welcome the new members. I also extend a warm welcome to all of you as we begin the 271stSession of the Rajya Sabha.

This House has a proud tradition of meaningful deliberations, and I am confident this Session will uphold that legacy. We have 19 sittings before us, and it is our collective responsibility to utilize this valuable time to debate and decide on issues of national importance.

Hon'ble Members, from this Session, the Secretariat has introduced live AI-based simultaneous interpretation of the proceedings in nine languages through the Multi-Media Devices at Members' seats. This facility will gradually be expanded to all twenty-two scheduled languages. Members are requested to share their feedback for its further improvement.

This Session has an important legislative and deliberative agenda. I urge Members to remain focused on the timely disposal of business, as every disruption delays discussion on matters affecting the nation and the people we represent.

I appeal to Members across all sides of the House to uphold the dignity, discipline and decorum of this august institution. While differences of opinion are integral to parliamentary democracy, they must be expressed in a manner that preserves the sanctity  of the House. Disruptions should not be allowed to undermine its functioning.

With these words, I wish you all a productive, constructive and purposeful Session.

 

****

RKK

(Release ID: 2286512)

Punj Ji, Shriman Swapan Sadhan Bose Ji, Shriman Rangasayee Ramakrishna Ji, Shriman H.
